questions.explanation: Robert Hooke discovered the cell in 1665 when he observed cork under a microscope. He saw box-like structures he called 'cells'.
Plant cells have a rigid cell wall made of cellulose outside the cell membrane. Animal cells do not have cell walls.

questions.text: What is the function of mitochondria?
questions.correctAnswer: Mitochondria produce energy (ATP) through cellular respiration.
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are called the 'powerhouse of the cell' because they generate ATP (adenosine triphosphate) through cellular respiration.

questions.text: The ________ is called the control center of the cell.
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains DNA and controls all cellular activities. It is called the control center or brain of the cell.
